assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements prepared under IFRS.
The FX exposure under discussion arises from an intragroup dividend receivable that has been recognised.
A hedging instrument or FX management instrument is being considered or has been used.

approaches.label: Hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes a general hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities, firm commitments and highly probable forecast transactions, which is directly relevant to a request to apply hedge documentation.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify net investment hedging as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Undesignated derivative accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 18 addresses presentation of gains and losses on derivatives that are not designated as hedging instruments under IFRS 9, making this a distinct alternative accounting model when hedge accounting is not applied.
